mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of months when FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 7.0%, deteriorating labor market conditions signal severe aggregate demand contraction, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 65% of months where UNRATE exceeds 7.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance with p-value greater than 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts signal a broad-based demand shock that forces equity repricing downward over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline condition yields positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses predictive power with t-statistic below 1.5 in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, a month-over-month increase in industrial production reflects stabilizing manufacturing output and supply chain normalization, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in less than 45% of months with rising INDPRO during recovery phases, or the signal reverses direction during acute liquidity crunches.

mechanism_narrative: When quarterly GDP growth falls below -2.0%, severe economic contraction triggers risk-off positioning and margin compression, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 55% of quarters where GDPC1 declines exceed 2.0%, or the threshold effect disappears after controlling for monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: A widening gap between the federal funds rate and core inflation indicates restrictive real rates that suppress corporate profitability, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The real rate spread shows no significant correlation with 60-day forward returns with Rank IC below 0.01, or positive returns dominate when real rates are high.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls signal strengthening economic activity and rising corporate revenues, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of months with payroll growth, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 1.0% signals acute manufacturing weakness that triggers immediate risk aversion and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are positive in more than 60% of months where INDPRO drops below the threshold, or the signal fails to survive transaction cost adjustments in live trading simulation.
